title: Tender Mustardy Malaysian Kabanos Sausages Tagine
description: A hearty one-pot meal featuring kabanos sausages, onions, and a blend of Malaysian spices.
instructions: Heat oil in the bottom of a large tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped red bell pepper and cook for another 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the curry powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the kabanos sausages and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until browned.
Add the unsweetened coconut milk, mustard seeds, and dark brown sugar and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the sausages are cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or scallions if desired.
Serve over rice or with naan bread for a filling meal.
This recipe makes 4-6 servings and can be easily doubled or tripled for a crowd.
Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
